title: SQL Analysis and Reporting
subtitle: Use SQL to calculate values, summarize rows, and build beginner reports.
learningGoals: Create calculated columns with arithmetic expressions.
Use aliases to make result columns readable.
Use beginner string, number, and date functions safely.
Understand how NULL affects expressions.
Use COUNT, SUM, AVG, MIN, and MAX.
Explain the difference between COUNT(*) and COUNT(column).
Use WHERE before aggregate calculations.
Group rows with GROUP BY.
Filter grouped results with HAVING.
Build small KPI and reporting queries.

modules.title: Calculated Columns and SQL Expressions
modules.purpose: Teach learners to create new values from existing data so raw tables become useful reports.

topics.title: What Expressions Are
topics.summary: Practice what expressions are so you can use it in a simple SQL query or workflow.
learningGoals: Use What Expressions Are in a simple SQL query or workflow.
Explain what result or output what expressions are should produce.

topics.title: Math in SQL
topics.summary: Practice math in SQL so you can use it in a simple SQL query or workflow.
learningGoals: Use Math in SQL in a simple SQL query or workflow.
Explain what result or output math in SQL should produce.

modules.title: Aggregate Functions
